title: Align existing-node reuse validation with merge provenance
problem: The extraction prompt says every edge endpoint must appear in delta nodes, including lightweight existing nodes, but validation allows edge endpoints from existing_nodes even when they are omitted from the delta. Merge only updates node paper_ids and ontology-status history when a node appears in the delta, so implicit existing endpoints can lose provenance.
proposed_change: Warn or reject implicit existing-node edge endpoints, validate existing/refined nodes against the current graph and preferably allowed_existing_nodes, and audit explicit reuse versus implicit endpoint reuse.
acceptance_criteria: A delta that references an existing graph node without a corresponding lightweight node produces a clear validation warning or error; reused existing/refined nodes are checked against the package graph context; audit reports explicit reuse, implicit endpoint reuse, and ontology-status conflicts.

observations.context: Subagent review found a prompt/validator/merge contract gap: prompts.py asks every edge endpoint to appear in nodes, validate.py accepts endpoints from ids|existing_nodes, and merge.py only updates node paper_ids when the node appears in the delta. Final run had 6 explicit multi-paper reused nodes and 6 ontology-status conflicts, so reuse provenance needs stricter accounting.
